{{commons category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} [[JA:村山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523149}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=August 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Murayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), Takarazuka-2.jpg|280px]]
Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名むらやましんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:斉明天皇]] [[Category:天智天皇]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. {{commons category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} [[JA:村山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523149}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=August 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Murayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), Takarazuka-2.jpg|280px]]
Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名むらやましんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:斉明天皇]] [[Category:天智天皇]][[Category:qq]]

Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名むらやましんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:斉明天皇]] [[Category:天智天皇]][[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} [[JA:村山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523149}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=August 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Murayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), Takarazuka-2.jpg|280px]]
Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:斉明天皇]] [[Category:天智天皇]][[Category:qq]]

Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:斉明天皇]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]][[Category:qq]]

Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:斉明天皇]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]][[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} [[JA:村山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523149}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=August 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Murayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), Takarazuka-2.jpg|280px]]
Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:斉明天皇]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]][[Category:qq]]

Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Saimei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]][[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. {{commons category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} [[JA:村山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523149}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=August 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Murayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), Takarazuka-2.jpg|280px]]
Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:四国中央市の建築物]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Saimei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|WD=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|WD=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|WD=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|WD=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|WD=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|WD=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:四国中央市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Shikoku Central]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Saimei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]]

Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|WD=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. {{commons category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} [[JA:村山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523149}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=August 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Murayama Shrine | 画像=[[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), Takarazuka-2.jpg|280px]]
Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|qid=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|qid=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|qid=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|qid=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|qid=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|qid=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|qid=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|qid=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|qid=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|qid=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|qid=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|qid=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|qid=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shikoku Central]] [[Category:Buildings in Shikoku Central]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Saimei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]]

Treasure mound (foreground) and haiden (rear right) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|qid=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}Doi-chō Tsune 1865 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|qid=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|qid=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|qid=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|qid=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|qid=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|qid=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|qid=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|qid=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|qid=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|qid=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|qid=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|qid=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shikoku Central]] [[Category:Buildings in Shikoku Central]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Saimei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|qid=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Doi-chō Tsune, {{ill|lt=Shikokuchūō|Q838666|qid=Q838666|1=Shikokuchūō}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-kōdaijin|1=Amaterasu}}. {{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|qid=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} are additionally enshrined. === Overview === Since shrine buildings and old records were burned in war fire in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|qid=Q6736|1=1585}}), the founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Empress Saimei and Emperor Tenji were merged here in September of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=674|Q30302|qid=Q30302|1=674}}). The shrine precinct is said to be the site of Iwase no Angū in Na-ōtsu, where Empress Saimei stayed while heading to Tsukushi to aid Baekje. There are also over 70 wooden statues, said to be attendants of Empress Saimei. A tradition also treats the "Otakarazuka" mound before the shrine as a mausoleum of a noble person. Generally, Na-ōtsu is identified with Hakata Bay and Iwase no Angū with Miyake in Minami-ku, Fukuoka City. However, this shrine's origin account cites a passage in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' saying "the imperial ship '''returned to''' Na-ōtsu and stayed at Iwase Angū," arguing that Empress Saimei returned by ship eastward from Iyu no Yukata ({{ill|lt=Dōgo Onsen|Q1037748|qid=Q1037748|1=Dōgo Onsen}}) in Iyo and stayed at this site. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 {{ill|lt=853|Q30367|qid=Q30367|1=853}}, it was included in the myōjin festival. In {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}) it rose to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and in Jōgan 12 ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|qid=Q23629|1=870}}) to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} it is listed as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. It is said that in former times pilgrimage to {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was called upper pilgrimage and worship at Murayama Shrine lower pilgrimage, and people from the thirty-three provinces of western Japan substituted worship at Murayama Shrine for pilgrimage to Ise. It was revered by the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|qid=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}} and the Matsudaira lords of {{ill|lt=Saijō Domain|Q2212226|qid=Q2212226|1=Saijō Domain}}, and in the Edo period was one of six prayer shrines of Saijō Domain together with {{ill|lt=Isono Shrine|Q11379396|qid=Q11379396|1=Isono Shrine}} and others. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), shinmon.jpg|Shinmon gate File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), horutonoki.jpg|Horuto tree File:Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo), keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festivals === {{ill|lt=Doi Taiko Festival|Q5288609|qid=Q5288609|1=Doi taikomatsuri}} [Dates: October 13, 14, 15] *Tsune Taikodai *Higashimura children's taikodai *Nishimura children's taikodai *Mitsuba-kai children's taikodai *Hinokuchi children's taikodai === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Murayama-jinja (Shikokuchuo)}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shikoku Central]] [[Category:Buildings in Shikoku Central]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Empress Saimei]] [[Category:Emperor Tenji]]
